reviews.review_msg: NOT a good instructor for this course! Could not learn anything from him! Waste of money and time
A very nice prof, choose him without any doubts.

Nice prof. BUS 100 was a joke anyway so having a caring guy lecture for an hour about how we should "chase our dreams" was honestly very enjoyable. At the very least, he was inspiring and was there to help with any questions.

He was my first business professor, and thus was my first impression of Beedie. He was more than just a lecturer; he was a figure of guidance. My friends and I felt a sense of belonging and pride for Beedie, thanks to him. Taught us material and taught us engagement alike. Great personality.

Stephen is very experienced and does not read off lecture slides. He adds on using examples. You don't really need the textbook, slides should be sufficient to do well in this course.   Only downside is you don't really know what to expect for exams and marking scheme for the exam is unclear.

Half way through the semester and I realized that Spector is not the guy I should have taken this course with. For someone who emphasizes that accounting is all about practice, but DOES NOT provide the solutions for assignment problems after the assignments have been graded, I feel he has lost touch of what it is like to be a student.

Excellent professor. He is technical but also encourages his students to think outside the box. He cares about his students and is very responsive to requests for help. Best accounting professor I have had.

He's a very good prof and tries to explain everything clearly. What I liked most about his class is that he doesn't give exams based on dead theory/memory-based questions. Instead, he gives you cases and you are expected to answer accordingly. Very useful if you plan on taking CASB. One of the best in SFU!

He is a nice guy at heart. However, the main issue is that he subconsciously stereotypes students and assigns grades accordingly. What I am saying is, it is impossible to get a high mark if you're Asian and quiet in the class. Try to get to know him personally, talk in class, and become one of his favorites and you will do well regardless.
